127582.explanation: Jong Zandvoort believes quality, competencies, and support base should guide the composition of the executive board. Diversity is important, but a mandatory gender-based distribution must never weigh heavier than suitability.

127583.explanation: Jong Zandvoort considers participation more important than a referendum. Residents should be involved early and actively through participation and advisory groups. A referendum is often too black-and-white and occurs only at the end of the process.

127584.explanation: Jong Zandvoort positively supports local ownership. If residents or associations can manage better, more efficiently, or with greater involvement, the municipality should facilitate this, provided quality, accessibility, and continuity are guaranteed.

127587.explanation: Jong Zandvoort supports local energy initiatives but financial support is not always the right solution. The municipality should primarily facilitate, remove obstacles, and focus on proven effective measures, providing targeted support where necessary.

127595.explanation: Jong Zandvoort wants a vibrant Zandvoort. Entrepreneurs and organizers should have the space to program major artists and DJs during the summer period. Stricter noise standards unnecessarily complicate events and harm the village's appeal.

127596.explanation: Jong Zandvoort believes everyone should be able to participate. Entrepreneurs who make their properties more accessible to people with disabilities make a social contribution. The municipality can stimulate this with targeted financial support or subsidies.

127598.explanation: Jong Zandvoort strongly supports this. Through the ZandvoortPas, we make sports and exercise accessible to residents with low incomes. Sport prevents exclusion, improves health, and strengthens social bonds.

127599.explanation: Jong Zandvoort finds local media indispensable for information provision and community connection. Initiatives like ZFM Zandvoort, Zandvoort Inside, and the Zandvoortse Courant deserve support to inform residents well and independently.

127601.explanation: Jong Zandvoort is certainly open to this if there is demand. Music lessons contribute to talent development and enjoyment. The municipality can facilitate this where requested, without making it mandatory.

127604.explanation: Jong Zandvoort wants significantly more enforcement officers, especially in the summer period on the boulevard. With extra powers, such as immediate action against nuisance and issuing fines, they can intervene directly and have a clear preventive effect. Nuisance, especially by groups of youths, must be addressed visibly and consistently.

127606.explanation: Jong Zandvoort believes victims must always be safely sheltered. If there is a need in Zandvoort for a women's shelter, this is open for discussion. Considerations should include scale, safety, and regional cooperation.

127607.explanation: Jong Zandvoort wants to give starters more opportunities in the housing market. A starter loan can make a difference for young residents who want to continue living in Zandvoort, provided this scheme is used in a targeted and financially responsible manner.

127610.explanation: Jong Zandvoort wants to tackle the housing shortage with common sense. New construction within the village is preferred, but if that is insufficient, building outside the village borders should not be automatically ruled out. Always with respect for nature and surroundings.

127611.explanation: Jong Zandvoort believes those who can should participate. Meaningful social work increases self-worth, routine, and involvement with the village. This must always suit the individual's capabilities, with room for customization and guidance.

127612.explanation: Jong Zandvoort wants to continue supporting residents with low incomes. Exemption from municipal taxes prevents financial problems and debts. This fits a social and fair policy, with attention to those who struggle financially.

127616.explanation: Jong Zandvoort currently opposes any change to the maximum speed within built-up areas. The current setup works, and a further reduction to 30 km/h is unnecessary. Tailored solutions can apply only where it is demonstrably unsafe.

127617.explanation: Jong Zandvoort believes good and accessible care for everyone is paramount. Given the current shortage of care personnel, it is undesirable to impose this as a strict requirement. Wishes can be considered where possible, but this must not limit care availability.

127620.explanation: Jong Zandvoort wants to keep Zandvoort attractive and affordable for visitors. A higher tourist tax makes stays more expensive and harms entrepreneurs. Residents should not bear all the burdens alone; extra taxes are not the right solution.

127621.explanation: Jong Zandvoort wants to provide space for local entrepreneurs and preserve employment in the village. Expanding business areas may be necessary to allow companies to grow, provided this is done carefully and balanced with livability and surroundings.

127623.explanation: Jong Zandvoort believes the scarce social rental homes should primarily be reserved for Zandvoort residents. Local young people must always have priority so they have the chance to remain living here and build their future.

127627.explanation: Jong Zandvoort considers it important that children grow up with knowledge of local culture and language. Attention to the Zandvoorts dialect in primary schools strengthens identity, connection, and pride in the village.

127630.explanation: Jong Zandvoort sees high-rise buildings as a way to address the housing shortage without consuming additional space. Tall residential buildings are possible in suitable locations, provided they fit the streetscape and contribute to quality, livability, and housing flow.

127632.explanation: Jong Zandvoort wants one large parking garage under the Badhuisplein/Favaugeplein for beachgoers and city center visitors. Additionally, we want to explore smart parking solutions in the center to relieve residential streets and maintain accessibility.

127634.explanation: Jong Zandvoort wants no changes to the current situation in Bentveld. There is no structural parking problem there. Paid parking is unnecessary and imposes extra burdens on residents, while not solving a non-existent problem.

127635.explanation: Jong Zandvoort opposes lowering the speed to 30 km/h on this main road. It is an important traffic artery for accessibility and flow. The current speed is appropriate; tailor-made measures apply only where safety is demonstrably compromised.

127637.explanation: Jong Zandvoort wants to encourage tourist rentals. Renting out single rooms or B&Bs up to 4 beds in one’s own home should be possible year-round, with the resident present. However, we oppose tourist rental of entire homes (without the resident present) to discourage investors and preserve homes for residents.

127582.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in our municipality. With an equal number of men and women, so that all perspectives are represented to make fair decisions.

127583.explanation: A referendum can be a beautiful and fair way to make a decision. GL-PvdA also wants to make important choices together with residents through citizen assemblies. This goes beyond a mere yes/no vote. Residents can also submit ideas, and together we strengthen local democracy.

127584.explanation: If you give people the trust to build something together in their own neighborhood, beautiful things arise. The municipality should not hinder this but rather encourage it.

127587.explanation: Energy cooperatives are an asset to our municipality. We have already proposed this in the municipality, and it has been adopted.

127595.explanation: Hearing protection and health are our starting points.

127596.explanation: Accessibility is vital for people with disabilities. However, many important places are not yet properly accessible, for example for wheelchair users. Entrepreneurs often cannot afford such adaptations on their own. Therefore, we believe the municipality should provide support.

127598.explanation: Sports give a lot of energy and satisfaction. And it keeps you healthy. But for people with welfare benefits, sports are often too expensive. We believe the municipality should ensure this group can participate in a sports club for free.

127599.explanation: Local media are extremely important to know what is happening in your own area, from the street to the municipal council. We believe the municipality should continue to contribute to this.

127601.explanation: Children are under huge pressure, from addictive social media to performance pressure at school. Music is the perfect outlet. We want music lessons not only to be accessible to children with wealthy parents. This way, we offer every child equal opportunities.

127604.explanation: Visible enforcement ensures safe neighborhoods. For example, nightlife coaches, public toilets staffed in the center, and crowd managers at crossings to the boulevard.

127606.explanation: If you no longer feel safe at home, there must be a place you can always go to. A women's shelter is indispensable for our municipality. It can also be located in a nearby municipality.

127607.explanation: The municipality should finance interest-free starter loans up to €30,000 and broaden the conditions.

127610.explanation: We want to preserve and protect the green spaces around our residential areas as best as possible.

127611.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

127612.explanation: If the cancellation of municipal taxes stops, the people with the lowest incomes in our municipality will face major problems. They simply cannot afford to lose that money. We believe everyone should be able to keep their head above water. Preventing debt is more important than collecting.

127616.explanation: The figures clearly show: a lower maximum speed makes traffic in our municipality much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with reduced mobility, this makes a huge difference. Moreover, the streets become quieter and the air cleaner.

127617.explanation: We believe that people should always have the freedom to choose.

127620.explanation: No tax increases without necessity. Tourist taxes should partially be used for municipal facilities. Better insight into expenditures is needed.

127621.explanation: This could be an option to improve the supply to the beach pavilions.

127623.explanation: Status holders have a residence permit and are therefore residents of Zandvoort. They have the right to priority. Above all, more social rental housing must be built.

127627.explanation: The Zandvoort dialect has almost disappeared but is certainly part of our cultural history. It is good if schools pay attention to our cultural history.

127630.explanation: We have an enormous housing shortage and action is needed. High-rise buildings either on the edges of Zandvoort or adding floors to existing flats. This way we make good use of scarce, valuable land. In all neighborhoods except Nieuw Noord. That area is too densely built with too many social housing flats.

127632.explanation: No expansion of on-street parking. But an underground parking garage at Favaugeplein and the Gasthuisplein and Raadhuisplein areas (combined with housing development at the Raadhuis location) will create more space for livability and green areas.

127634.explanation: The nuisance caused by beach visitors and stay tourists must be reduced. For this, paid parking is needed in Bentveld as well, just like in the rest of Zandvoort.

127635.explanation: The figures clearly show: with a lower maximum speed, traffic in our municipality becomes much safer. Additionally, the streets are quieter and the air cleaner.

127637.explanation: The 120-day rental allowance for your own home will be reduced to 30 days in 2027 to counteract housing withdrawal. Renting out a room (Zimmer frei) remains always possible.

127582.explanation: We strongly support both women and men in the College and more generally, more women in politics. Regardless of the man/woman question, our basis is always the quality of the candidates and the parties' ability to propose the right alderman.

127583.explanation: A referendum is a means for exceptional issues. Much more important for more everyday topics is a good participation policy and proactively involving residents in decision-making on important topics to create support.

127584.explanation: The government should facilitate more than implement. So, organizing a sports hall through a foundation or association is a good development. Neighborhoods can work on strengthening their own district by adopting, for example, a park.

127587.explanation: We support the option of loans with a favorable interest rate that are repaid in installments. This way, many more people can benefit because it is a fund that replenishes itself repeatedly.

127595.explanation: We have good rules. Enforcement requires more attention.

127596.explanation: This is the responsibility of entrepreneurs. However, the municipality itself must set a good example in public spaces.

127598.explanation: We have established a sports and culture fund which has taken care of this in Zandvoort.

127599.explanation: No EXTRA money is needed now, but we must ensure there is sufficient funding available for the local broadcaster.

127601.explanation: We have established a sports and culture fund which has taken care of this in Zandvoort.

127604.explanation: Certainly more focus on integrated enforcement with possibilities for actual intervention.

127606.explanation: To fulfill the purpose of a women's shelter, physical distance from offenders is necessary as long as they have not been convicted, and for that Zandvoort is simply too small. We collaborate regionally on femicide and have an action plan.

127607.explanation: We have a starter loan and it works well.

127610.explanation: At the moment, we have no choice. We are completely surrounded by Natura 2000 areas. The municipality has no say over this.

127611.explanation: Recipients of social assistance benefits generally do not choose to rely on this aid. Depending on the person's situation and possibilities, it is good for them to contribute to society, but care must be taken not to frame this as a 'punishment'.

127612.explanation: We have good arrangements and it is necessary that these residents receive extra support.

127616.explanation: This is already common on many roads, but traffic flow must be guaranteed.

127617.explanation: It concerns the quality of care. The answer is therefore no.

127620.explanation: Because costs rise yearly, indexing is desirable.

127621.explanation: Unfortunately, space is limited; we are a tourist municipality as well as a residential area, so with limited space available we prefer more housing rather than more commercial space.

127623.explanation: We distribute the scarcity appropriately but must take into account the requirements set by the national government.

127627.explanation: There is a Zandvoort dialect, but it is passed on from parent to child. It is not a separate language.

127630.explanation: Our current skyline is high enough. We also focus more on quality living rather than just quantity.

127632.explanation: There is insufficient space for more parking spaces. We focus on parking in the lots at the edges of the village.

127634.explanation: If it is necessary to encourage tourists who park there to use the central parking lots, introducing paid parking in Bentveld is one possible method, but this must be investigated first, along with alternative regulations.

127635.explanation: Keeping the through route from Bloemdaal at a minimum of 50 km/h is necessary to maintain optimal traffic flow and travel times.

127637.explanation: Will be implemented as of January 1, 2027.

127582.explanation: Quality and reliability are more important than gender.

127584.explanation: Small tasks such as maintaining a park or greenery around a tree are fine, but building a sports hall is too extensive.

127587.explanation: They should be able to get support from the municipality, but not financial support.

127595.explanation: This is already arranged and realized through participation with entrepreneurs and residents.

127596.explanation: This is not a task of the municipality when it concerns private property.

127598.explanation: For this, we have the ZandvoortPass and there are options through the sports and culture fund.

127599.explanation: The municipality already financially supports the local broadcaster, but if extra is needed, it can be provided.

127601.explanation: For children whose parents cannot afford it financially, there are sufficient possibilities to support them.

127604.explanation: Safety and quality must come first. If that means we need more BOAs (neighborhood enforcement officers), we are in favor. But let's first see if the current BOAs can better divide their tasks. Bring back neighborhood police officers to the neighborhoods as well!

127606.explanation: This is a regional task. We have no room for it. Zandvoort residents have been on the housing waiting list for years.

127607.explanation: We have the starter's loan. We want it to be used only for Zandvoort residents, not for people coming from outside.

127610.explanation: We support building outside the borders. But Zandvoort is surrounded by Natura 2000 areas. Building there is prohibited by national regulations.

127611.explanation: They may certainly contribute to society, provided they are obviously able to do so. Volunteers are very important and necessary.

127612.explanation: We must help people who truly need it.

127616.explanation: 30 km/h is fine in neighborhoods. Main roads should remain at 50 km/h, also for emergency response times.

127617.explanation: There are insufficient care providers. Choosing someone who matches your faith, culture, or beliefs is not a right, but a preference.

127620.explanation: The tourist tax is already high enough. Zandvoort should not price itself out of the market.

127623.explanation: Zandvoort residents have been waiting for years for housing. They do not get priority either.

127627.explanation: The culture and history of Zandvoort are important. Extra attention to the Zandvoort dialect is not necessary.

127630.explanation: Zandvoort should not become the seaside Schalkwijk. We must cherish the village character.

127632.explanation: Too many parking spaces are lost during reorganization.

127634.explanation: We must consult with the residents of Bentveld to ask if they experience nuisance and what solution they find best for Bentveld.

127635.explanation: Main roads should remain at 50 km/h, also because of emergency response times.

127637.explanation: Livability in neighborhoods is most important. Renting out an entire house is only permitted if you live there yourself. Renting out a second home for recreation is prohibited. Bed & Breakfast is part of Zandvoort. We support a study regarding the number of rental days.

127582.explanation: The VVD supports diversity. A strong executive board therefore contains a diversity of knowledge and expertise.

127583.explanation: Many issues are complex and unfortunately cannot be answered with a simple 'yes' or 'no'.

127584.explanation: Social participation and involvement are important to the Zandvoort VVD. Residents who want to roll up their sleeves must have the opportunity to do so.

127587.explanation: The municipality is positive about an energy cooperative. The Zandvoort VVD believes the municipality should only facilitate and not finance such initiatives.

127595.explanation: The standards for festivals and parties were recently adjusted in 2025 when establishing Zandvoort's event policy. The Zandvoort VVD sees no need to change the noise standards now.

127596.explanation: The Zandvoort VVD believes entrepreneurs should decide how they conduct their business. Accessibility for all customers is self-evident.

127598.explanation: The municipality currently supports low-income families by paying for children's sports lessons and there are arrangements with the ZandvoortPass. We see no need to expand this.

127599.explanation: The Zandvoort VVD supports the local broadcaster. Currently, we see no need to allocate extra funds.

127601.explanation: The municipality currently has sufficient programs to support children when needed.

127604.explanation: The Zandvoort VVD is a strong supporter of a safe Zandvoort. This means sufficient visible enforcement on the streets.

127606.explanation: Municipality Zandvoort is too small to offer these facilities at the proper level. Therefore, we have agreements with Haarlem.

127607.explanation: The Zandvoort VVD gladly helps all residents to obtain housing.

127610.explanation: The existing village boundaries consist of the dune area (Natura 2000). The Zandvoort VVD is not in favor of building in the dune area.

127611.explanation: The Zandvoort VVD finds it important that everyone participates. This is good for the self-esteem of its residents.

127612.explanation: The Zandvoort VVD believes that everyone who uses services, such as waste collection, should pay for them like everyone else.

127616.explanation: Safety must be the priority when choosing the speed limit on a road. Roads like Van Lennepweg and the boulevard are not suitable for 30 km/h.

127617.explanation: Care provided must match the problems and complaints of residents. The Zandvoort VVD believes that other criteria should not play a role in choosing the best care.

127620.explanation: The current tourist tax has not been increased in line with inflation for several years. The Zandvoort VVD wants to use the additional revenue from the tourist tax to enhance livability in Zandvoort and to distribute the costs of waste collection more fairly.

127621.explanation: The Zandvoort VVD supports giving businesses space. We also see opportunities to expand the business park in Nieuw-Noord along the railway.

127623.explanation: The Zandvoort VVD believes that Zandvoort residents should have priority for social rental homes.

127627.explanation: The Zandvoort VVD finds it important that all children learn about the environment in which they grow up. Zandvoort’s culture is also part of that.

127630.explanation: The Zandvoort VVD finds tower blocks unsuitable for the character of the village.

127632.explanation: The Zandvoort VVD would like to see an increase in the number of (underground) parking spaces.

127634.explanation: The Zandvoort VVD sees no reason to introduce paid parking in Bentveld.

127635.explanation: The Zandvoort VVD wants to maintain the maximum speed of 60 km/h on Boulevard Barnaart.

127637.explanation: The Zandvoort VVD believes that renting out an entire home should be limited to a maximum of 30 days. Zimmer Frei and B&Bs should be allowed year-round.

127582.explanation: Equal opportunities are important, but mandatory quotas are not. The council must be formed based on quality, suitability, and support. That should always be leading.

127583.explanation: In important decisions about the future of Zandvoort, residents must be able to make their voices heard. Participation starts with those directly involved and can be expanded step-by-step. A referendum can be a clear democratic instrument for major and impactful choices.

127584.explanation: The municipality must give residents space to think along and participate. But public facilities like a sports hall are not an experiment. Responsibility remains with the municipality. Encouraging initiatives, yes; shifting tasks away, no.

127587.explanation: Sustainability is important, but not at any cost. The municipality should not let residents bear financial risks of energy projects. Support where necessary is possible, but no blank checks.

127595.explanation: Zandvoort is a seaside resort where events belong. Noise standards have been recently established. What’s important is that these are well enforced. Stricter rules are not needed, but clear agreements and compliance are.

127596.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate. Making a building accessible is primarily the responsibility of the entrepreneur. At the same time, the municipality has a clear responsibility for its own buildings and public spaces. The municipality must set a good example.

127598.explanation: Sport is not a luxury but helps people participate in society. Residents on social assistance must also be able to play sports. Especially children from socially weaker families must not be sidelined. Support must be targeted and responsible, without an open-ended scheme.

127599.explanation: Free and independent local media are essential. We fully recognize that role. Extra subsidies without clear necessity are unwise. Support where needed, but no blank check. Media must not become dependent on municipal financial involvement.

127601.explanation: Music education is valuable, but general subsidies for everyone are not a core task of the municipality. Support should target families who truly need it. Municipal funds must prioritize safety, care, and housing.

127604.explanation: Visible enforcement is essential for safety and livability. We want structurally more enforcement officers, so that nuisance can be tackled faster and rules better enforced all year round. Enforcement is necessary, but issuing fines is not an end in itself. Safety and order are.

127606.explanation: Victims of domestic violence must always be able to find immediate safety. That is paramount. In a small municipality like Zandvoort, having a local shelter is not always the safest solution. That is why we choose strong regional cooperation with specialized shelters and professional guidance.

127607.explanation: A starter loan sounds attractive, but it does not make houses cheaper, it increases debt. We choose real solutions: faster construction, affordable homes, and fewer regulations. That is what really helps starters.

127610.explanation: Housing shortages require realism, not blockades. First build within the village, but do not exclude anything in advance as long as nature and legislation are respected. We must seek and utilize the limits of possibilities. Zandvoort residents have the right to perspective on housing.

127611.explanation: A benefit is a safety net, not a final destination. Those who can contribute must do so through socially meaningful work. For people who truly cannot, tailor-made solutions apply. Participation should be the norm.

127612.explanation: People who really need help must be assisted. At the same time, waiver of payment should not be automatic. We choose tailor-made support: help where needed and policies that do not discourage working and earning extra income.

127616.explanation: A general reduction to 30 km/h throughout the entire built-up area is not wise. Safety requires tailored approaches. In residential neighborhoods 30 km/h may be logical, but main roads and access roads to Zandvoort must remain accessible and flowing.

127617.explanation: Care must focus on quality, availability, and accessibility. Additional requirements based on religion or beliefs should not restrict access to care. Everyone, especially vulnerable families and children, must be able to receive the right help in time.

127620.explanation: Tourism is important for Zandvoort and provides much employment. The tourist tax is already at a proper level and further increases could harm our competitive position compared to abroad. VAT on accommodation has already increased from 9% to 21%.

127621.explanation: Zandvoort has limited space. That must first be used for homes for its own residents and for quality of life. Entrepreneurs are important, but expanding business parks is not a priority now. First organize existing locations more cleverly and divide land better, for example around the municipal depot.

127623.explanation: Zandvoort residents have been waiting for social housing for years. Extra priority for one group from outside increases pressure on an already tense housing market. Homes are there to give residents perspective, not to create new inequality and thus unrest in society.

127627.explanation: Proud of our village? Certainly. But education should not become a political instrument. Schools determine their own curriculum. Attention to local culture is allowed, but the municipality should not impose this.

127630.explanation: We are not for or against high-rise buildings as a principle. Housing is needed but must fit the environment and village character. Smart densification is possible, for example during restructuring in Nieuw Noord, provided it is careful and appropriate. Faster building, fewer rules.

127632.explanation: An accessible center is essential for residents and entrepreneurs. Additional parking spaces, smartly and responsibly realized partly underground, can relieve residential streets and ensure a good balance between accessibility and livability.

127634.explanation: Paid parking should not become automatic. If there is nuisance, it must be tackled specifically. Introducing extra costs for residents without clear necessity is not a solution.

127635.explanation: 30 km/h belongs in residential streets, not on an important traffic artery. We choose safe solutions where necessary but keep the coastal route accessible for residents, visitors, and emergency services.

127637.explanation: We oppose a general 30-day rental limit. Private renting is a legitimate source of income for many residents and Zandvoort has partly grown because of individual rentals. Nuisance must be tackled through targeted enforcement, not by collective measures for everyone. Freedom where possible, action where necessary.

127582.explanation: Where possible, of course D66 still focuses on quality.

127583.explanation: D66 has also already drafted a bill to ensure that topics important to residents must be discussed during council meetings.

127584.explanation: If the government fails or residents can simply do it better.

127587.explanation: D66 has also submitted a motion, which was adopted, to support energy cooperatives as much as possible.

127595.explanation: In Zandvoort, there is already good enforcement of noise standards. A new proposal was recently adopted in 2025.

127596.explanation: Entrepreneurs are perfectly capable of managing that themselves.

127598.explanation: D66 believes that people should move as much as possible. This helps preventively and fosters a positive mindset. Additionally, it offers opportunities for equal chances and social contact, which can lead to job opportunities.

127599.explanation: Where necessary, to ensure that the residents of Zandvoort continue to receive the local news.

127601.explanation: This allows children to develop new talents they didn't know they had.

127604.explanation: More BOAs (special enforcement officers) do not automatically lead to more safety. Smart deployment of BOAs combined with preventive measures helps maintain safety.

127606.explanation: D66 prefers to leave this to the experts.

127607.explanation: D66 has also achieved this in Zandvoort.

127610.explanation: D66 believes that broad solutions must be sought to address the tightness on the housing market.

127611.explanation: This is already regulated by law.

127612.explanation: Debt forgiveness can work very well to give everyone a decent livelihood. Additionally, the municipality must increase efforts in prevention and properly inform residents about taking out loans and managing money. Many problems have other causes.

127616.explanation: This leads to less pressure, less noise, less emissions, and increased safety. All to keep Zandvoort livable for its residents.

127617.explanation: This is also part of an inclusive society. However, the provision must be available. If it is not, then people must accept the existing options.

127620.explanation: The tax has already been raised sufficiently at this moment.

127621.explanation: Currently, the municipal council has chosen for a mixed industrial site, combining living and working. Simply put, there is little space left for further expansion.

127623.explanation: This is also regulated by law. The same rights apply to every Dutch citizen.

127627.explanation: It is good to know what used to be in Zandvoort. Explanation and history make the village better known.

127630.explanation: D66 believes that building should largely happen upwards, while fitting within the environmental plan and spatial appearance.

127632.explanation: The question is whether this can and should be done. D66 thinks that through smart design, more greenery can be added without reducing the number of parking spaces. In addition, there are possibilities to expand parking garages.

127634.explanation: D66 has always pointed this out. Parking pressure acts like an oil slick, pushing people more towards the free options.

127635.explanation: This leads to less pressure, less noise, less emissions, and increased safety. All to keep Zandvoort livable for its residents.

127637.explanation: D66 wants a minimum period of 60 days and good and adequate enforcement of this.

127582.explanation: Equal opportunities are essential, but enforcing equal outcomes is not. As long as the people of Zandvoort are substantively represented, the composition does not matter.

127583.explanation: The referendum is a spearhead of ZEE. We believe residents should have the final say on major decisions, not just the council. We even submitted a proposal for this as the only party. Strikingly, parties that claim to support referenda voted against it.

127584.explanation: The right to challenge has already been implemented in Zandvoort and rightly is not an election issue because there is broad satisfaction about it. If a resident can and wants to do it better, we must provide that space.

127587.explanation: ZEE took the initiative to facilitate an energy cooperative so that residents can maintain influence over their energy without leading to fragmentation of projects and so that people with lower incomes can benefit from lower energy costs.

127595.explanation: Zandvoort is a village where tourism and events are central. Too strict standards do not do justice to the character of our village.

127596.explanation: ZEE supports municipal assistance with advice on making shops and restaurants wheelchair-friendly, and joint procurement of facilities such as wheelchair ramps, so that costs for both entrepreneurs and the municipality are lower.

127598.explanation: ZEE wants sport to be accessible to everyone. A membership promotes health, social connection, and participation in society, especially for people with social assistance benefits.

127599.explanation: ZEE is in favor of preserving local media such as radio, television, and newspapers, provided they can operatively remain independent.

127601.explanation: ZEE finds it important to offer help where it is most urgently needed. Given the current demand for help, we therefore do not consider it wise to facilitate music lessons for all primary school children.

127604.explanation: ZEE wants broader deployment of security in the evening and night, with extra capacity at events. We want to jointly procure security together with hospitality entrepreneurs, on the beach and in the village, so that supervision is more efficient and costs and responsibilities are fairly distributed.

127606.explanation: These facilities already exist and we want to maintain them. Women from Zandvoort cannot access these in their own municipality and depend on shelters elsewhere. By continuing to support these facilities, appropriate shelter remains available for our residents when needed.

127607.explanation: ZEE considers starter loans unsafe because they accumulate debt without lowering housing prices. We prefer social homeownership: homes remain structurally affordable, risks are lower, and housing turnover remains possible, while Zandvoort residents do get a chance at ownership.

127610.explanation: This is not an issue in Zandvoort because our village boundaries are determined by heavily protected Natura 2000 areas and the sea, where building is simply not possible.

127611.explanation: ZEE encourages socially meaningful work, but not everyone with social assistance benefits is able to do so, therefore we do not want to make it mandatory.

127612.explanation: Ending the forgiveness of these costs increases financial pressure on people with low incomes, which benefits no one.

127616.explanation: ZEE wants 30 km/h as the standard on roads within built-up areas where living, cycling, and crossing come together. Lower speeds reduce traffic jams, accidents, and noise nuisance, creating a more pleasant streetscape. Roads with 50 km/h must be demonstrably safely designed.

127617.explanation: This choice already exists. People can choose a healthcare provider that suits them. The statement suggests a problem that does not exist in practice.

127620.explanation: Hotels have recently already faced higher national taxes and costs. A further increase in the tourist tax places additional pressure on a sector that already contributes disproportionately and harms Zandvoort's competitive position.

127621.explanation: Zandvoort must handle the scarce building land carefully. However, to realize the Curiedex project and thereby build more than 600 homes, a limited expansion of the industrial area on the north side, along the railway, is necessary for the relocation of storage.

127623.explanation: ZEE opposes this. Due to the housing shortage, every social rental home is urgently needed for Zandvoort residents. Additional immigration increases the pressure on the housing market and makes the problem bigger rather than smaller.

127627.explanation: ZEE supports attention to and archiving of Zandvoort's culture and history, but this should not be part of the standard school curriculum.

127630.explanation: For ZEE, this depends on the context. We would actually like to see the center and boulevard area more in a seaside resort style with generally lower buildings. In other locations, such as the North Boulevard, this could be proportional to the other buildings and, if attractive, even higher for more homes.

127632.explanation: Certain neighborhoods in Zandvoort show a structural shortage of parking spaces. Therefore, ZEE supports the creation of more parking spaces.

127634.explanation: ZEE sees the increasing parking pressure in Bentveld as a direct consequence of the parking issues in Zandvoort. Therefore, we support jointly investigating with residents which form of paid parking best suits Bentveld.

127635.explanation: In this area there is no noise nuisance and little crossing traffic. For good traffic flow, 60 km/h speed limits and road widening are necessary.

127637.explanation: Tourist rentals are already limited to 30 days per year. ZEE opposed this. Tackling nuisance is justified, but reducing the limit from 120 to 30 days punishes residents unnecessarily and deprives them of income that actually provides space for relaxation. Therefore, ZEE advocates for 80 days.

statements.title: Zandvoort should limit tourist rentals of homes to 30 nights per year.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
Business parks in Zandvoort should be allowed to expand.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Zandvoort should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: More high-rise residential buildings should be allowed in Zandvoort.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: Residents of Zandvoort should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Zandvoort moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should introduce paid parking in Bentveld.
There should be a women's shelter (domestic violence refuge) in Zandvoort.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: More parking spaces should be created in the center.
The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include attention to the Zandvoort dialect.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The speed limit on the main coastal route through Zandvoort should be 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.

titleParts.text: op de doorgaande route langs de kust van Zandvoort moet 30 km/u worden.
